title: Listening - Part 3: Choosing a Holiday Activity
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about an activity they chose to do while on holiday. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the activity. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about an activity they chose to do while on holiday. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the activity. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about choosing a holiday activity.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I was staying on an island known for beautiful beaches, and most people in my hotel were satisfied with swimming and sunbathing. I enjoyed that too, but I had read that dolphins were often seen further out at sea. [19]I booked a boat trip because I wanted the chance to watch marine animals living naturally in the area rather than only reading about them in a guidebook.[/19] We eventually saw several dolphins following the boat. The trip started very early, but I would happily have got up even earlier for that experience.||Speaker 2 Man: During our holiday in southern Spain, temperatures became so high after lunch that walking around outdoors was exhausting. We'd already visited cafés and didn't want to spend another afternoon inside our hotel room. [20]We chose a guided tour of underground caves because it would let us explore somewhere interesting while staying naturally cool during the hottest part of the day.[/20] The rock formations were far more impressive than I expected. In fact, even on a cooler day, I think the visit would have been worthwhile.||Speaker 3 Woman: I was travelling in Morocco and saw several cooking workshops advertised. At first I wasn't sure, since I can easily find recipes online and had limited holiday time. [21]I finally joined one held in a family home because it offered a chance to hear about everyday meals, shopping and traditions from local people rather than simply eating dishes in restaurants.[/21] We prepared bread and soup together, and the conversation while cooking taught me as much as the recipe itself.||Speaker 4 Man: My sister and I rented a cottage with her two children. The weather forecast included several rainy days, and we knew they would become restless if we expected them to spend every afternoon reading indoors. [22]We booked a visit to an indoor adventure centre because it had climbing areas, water games and activities suitable for both children, giving them an exciting day even if the weather was awful.[/22] It did rain all day, and they returned tired and happy, so the adults enjoyed a quiet evening.||Speaker 5 Woman: My job involves answering messages and checking screens throughout the day, and I noticed that I was doing the same thing on holiday whenever I had a spare moment. A hotel offered a day-long guided walk in the mountains. [23]I chose it because there was no phone signal on the route and I wanted one whole day when I couldn't check emails or disappear into online updates.[/23] At first I reached for my phone automatically, but by lunchtime I had stopped thinking about it and simply enjoyed walking.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I was staying on an island known for beautiful beaches, and most people in my hotel were satisfied with swimming and sunbathing. I enjoyed that too, but I had read that dolphins were often seen further out at sea. I booked a boat trip because I wanted the chance to watch marine animals living naturally in the area rather than only reading about them in a guidebook. We eventually saw several dolphins following the boat. The trip started very early, but I would happily have got up even earlier for that experience.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: During our holiday in southern Spain, temperatures became so high after lunch that walking around outdoors was exhausting. We'd already visited cafés and didn't want to spend another afternoon inside our hotel room. We chose a guided tour of underground caves because it would let us explore somewhere interesting while staying naturally cool during the hottest part of the day. The rock formations were far more impressive than I expected. In fact, even on a cooler day, I think the visit would have been worthwhile.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I was travelling in Morocco and saw several cooking workshops advertised. At first I wasn't sure, since I can easily find recipes online and had limited holiday time. I finally joined one held in a family home because it offered a chance to hear about everyday meals, shopping and traditions from local people rather than simply eating dishes in restaurants. We prepared bread and soup together, and the conversation while cooking taught me as much as the recipe itself.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: My sister and I rented a cottage with her two children. The weather forecast included several rainy days, and we knew they would become restless if we expected them to spend every afternoon reading indoors. We booked a visit to an indoor adventure centre because it had climbing areas, water games and activities suitable for both children, giving them an exciting day even if the weather was awful. It did rain all day, and they returned tired and happy, so the adults enjoyed a quiet evening.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My job involves answering messages and checking screens throughout the day, and I noticed that I was doing the same thing on holiday whenever I had a spare moment. A hotel offered a day-long guided walk in the mountains. I chose it because there was no phone signal on the route and I wanted one whole day when I couldn't check emails or disappear into online updates. At first I reached for my phone automatically, but by lunchtime I had stopped thinking about it and simply enjoyed walking.

options.text: to learn about the local way of life
to improve a skill they already had
to spend time away from technology
to keep younger family members entertained

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker selected the boat trip in order to see dolphins in their natural surroundings (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The caves offered an interesting activity away from the extreme outdoor heat (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The workshop gave the speaker direct insight into local food traditions and everyday life (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The activity centre was chosen to keep the children active and amused during bad weather (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker deliberately chose a mountain walk as a break from constant phone and internet use (evidence [23]).
